plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability. Add to CRWD, a high-scoring quality tech name. Initiate a small position in TSLA, a top scanner setup.

This plan focuses on capital preservation, reducing overall portfolio risk and leverage. It prioritizes broad market exposure and income-generating assets. A significant cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
conservative.read: This mode targets approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and low drawdown. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure. Increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income and stability. Add to CRWD, a quality tech name with a good scanner score. Initiate a small position in NVDU, a strong scanner setup.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a diversified portfolio with reduced leverage. It aims for steady growth while adhering to risk limits. A moderate cash buffer will be maintained.
balanced.read: This mode targets approximately 2.5% per month, balancing risk and growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, offering a more stable growth path with moderate drawdown potential.

moves.note: Add to leveraged semis, high score.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate. Add to SOXL, a leveraged semiconductor ETF with a high scanner score. Sell SPAL to exit a losing leveraged position. Add significantly to CRWD, a strong performer with a high score. Initiate a larger position in TSLA, a top scanner setup.

This plan aggressively leans into high-scoring setups and leverages for growth. It accepts higher volatility and potential drawdowns. Strict stop discipline is crucial due to leveraged ETF decay and rebalancing.
aggressive.read: This mode targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for aggressive growth. It involves higher leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential for larger drawdowns, especially with leveraged ETFs.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF risk. Significantly increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income focus. Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth exposure and favor income. Initiate a small position in CVX, an income-generating equity to diversify.

This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. It minimizes exposure to high-beta single names and leveraged products. A substantial cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
income.read: This mode targets approximately 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns. This is not a savings account.
